data.title: Sub-agent retry budget with model escalation
data.description:
## User Story
As a sprint orchestrator, I want task descriptions to include a retry budget with sequential sonnet->opus model escalation so failing sub-agents don't spiral indefinitely.

## Done Definitions
1. Task descriptions generated by implementation-plan include retry budget: MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3 on sonnet model, then MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3 on opus model with diagnostic context from previous sonnet failures
2. After 6 total attempts (3 sonnet + 3 opus), escalate to user with full failure history
3. When global throttle reports MAX_AGENTS: 0 at time of sonnet->opus escalation, opus step is skipped and user escalation occurs immediately
4. Sprint orchestrator parses and respects the retry budget embedded in task descriptions, including explicit modifications to sprint SKILL.md Phase 4 dispatch logic to parse MAX_ATTEMPTS. fix-bug uses its existing tiered escalation (Tier 1 sonnet -> Tier 2 opus -> Tier 3 bot-psychologist) and is NOT modified by this story.
5. Opus escalation prompt includes diagnostic context: failure messages from the 3 sonnet attempts
6. sprint SKILL.md Phase 4 dispatch logic modification explicitly lists which fields it reads from task descriptions, including MAX_ATTEMPTS
7. Tests: structural boundary test validates retry budget format in task description; integration test (mocked): given 3 consecutive sonnet failures, orchestrator initiates opus escalation with diagnostic context; given MAX_AGENTS: 0 at escalation time, orchestrator escalates to user directly

## Considerations
- [Reliability] If opus fails all 3 cycles, user escalation must include full diagnostic context without data loss

## Escalation Policy
Escalate when blocked: Agents proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. All assumptions made without escalating are documented.
